title: quercus baetica
intro: Quercus baetica, also known as the Andalusian oak, is a deciduous tree native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its high tannin and polyphenol content. The medicinal properties of Quercus baetica have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against certain diseases. Quercus baetica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some herbal supplements due to its potential health benefits.
